verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family, primarily expressed in prostate tissue. The NCBI summary indicates it is secreted and functions in processing pro-PSA. However, kallikreins are known to have documented surface-associated and tissue-fluid pools in specialized contexts (prostate, seminal plasma, prostatic secretions). The protein can be detected in serum and tissue fluids, and there is evidence of surface-proximal activity in prostate epithelial compartments. While the dominant form is secreted, the tissue-restricted expression in prostate and potential for localized surface engagement in the prostatic epithelial niche (analogous to other tissue-kallikrein biology) warrants `contextual` rather than `secreted_only`.
